query: MemeCore M token news late June 2026 - flash crash aftermath, exchange delistings, Binance perpetual contract status, ZachXBT criticism
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of what I found on the MemeCore (M) token, late June 2026:

## Key Findings

**The Flash Crash (June 25, 2026):** M plunged ~74–80% from ~$2.92 to an intraday low of ~$0.51 (stabilizing $0.68–$0.74), erasing nearly **$3 billion** in value on just **~$21 million** in volume — with no hack, exploit, or announcement to explain it.

**"Ghost Market Cap" structure:** Over 90% insider-held supply, under $100,000 in on-chain liquidity, and zero transfers above $50,000 on the native chain for 2+ weeks prior. FDV collapsed from a peak near $14B to ~$3.8B.

**Binance perpetual contract status — important nuance:** I found **no confirmed delisting** of M (spot or perpetual) by any exchange in the late-June window. "Delisting" appears only as:
1. **April 2026 rumors** of Binance futures delisting (drove an earlier ~8% dip), and
2. **Post-crash demands** from ZachXBT for Binance and Bybit to explain/reconsider the perp listings.

Binance and Bybit offered M **perpetual futures** (Bybit up to 50x); Kraken and Bitget offered spot.

**ZachXBT criticism:** He first flagged M on April 20, 2026 (citing $7.9M in suspicious Kraken withdrawals to 18 new addresses, alleging manipulation to ~$6B cap/$18B FDV). On crash day, with analysts Mlm and Wazz, he said the red flags had "caught up" and publicly demanded answers from all four exchanges, while confronting MemeCore figure Rudy Rong on X. His manipulation claims remain **allegations**, not regulator-verified.

The full prose report and structured data (key facts, sources, data points) have been submitted.

sources.title: MemeCore M Token Falls 75%, ZachXBT Asks Four Exchanges How It Passed Review

sources.snippet: M shed 76%, falling from ~$2.66 to ~$0.50 intraday, trading near $0.6858 at press time; market cap below $1B.
